children.heading: Sale, servicing, and leasing of motor vehicles excluded
children.label: a Sale, servicing, and leasing of motor vehicles excluded
children.text: Except as permitted in subsection (b), the Bureau may not exercise any rulemaking, supervisory, enforcement or any other authority, including any authority to order assessments, over a motor vehicle dealer that is predominantly engaged in the sale and servicing of motor vehicles, the leasing and servicing of motor vehicles, or both.
provides consumers with any services related to residential or commercial mortgages or self-financing transactions involving real property;

children.label: A
children.text: that involves the extension of retail credit or retail leases involving motor vehicles; and

children.label: i
children.text: the extension of retail credit or retail leases are provided directly to consumers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the contract governing such extension of retail credit or retail leases is not routinely assigned to an unaffiliated third party finance or leasing source; or

offers or provides a consumer financial product or service not involving or related to the sale, financing, leasing, rental, repair, refurbishment, maintenance, or other servicing of motor vehicles, motor vehicle parts, or any related or ancillary product or service.

children.heading: Preservation of authorities of other agencies
children.label: c Preservation of authorities of other agencies
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (b) and (d), nothing in this title, including subtitle F, shall be construed as modifying, limiting, or superseding the operation of any provision of Federal law, or otherwise affecting the authority of the Board of Governors, the Federal Trade Commission, or any other Federal agency, with respect to a person described in subsection (a).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Federal Trade Commission authority
children.label: d Federal Trade Commission authority
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Federal Trade Commission is authorized to prescribe rules under sections 45 and 57a(a)(1)(B) of title 15. in accordance with , with respect to a person described in subsection (a).section 57a of title 152section 553 of title 5So in original. The period probably should be a comma.2

children.heading: Coordination with Office of Service Member Affairs
children.label: e Coordination with Office of Service Member Affairs

children.text: service members and their families are educated and empowered to make better informed decisions regarding consumer financial products and services offered by motor vehicle dealers, with a focus on motor vehicle dealers in the proximity of military installations; and

complaints by service members and their families concerning such motor vehicle dealers are effectively monitored and responded to, and where appropriate, enforcement action is pursued by the authorized agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: any self-propelled vehicle designed for transporting persons or property on a street, highway, or other road;

children.label: D
children.text: motor homes, recreational vehicle trailers, and slide-in campers, as those terms are defined in sections 571.3 and 575.103 (d) of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor thereto; and

children.label: E
children.text: other vehicles that are titled and sold through dealers.

children.label: A
children.text: is licensed by a State, a territory of the United States, or the District of Columbia to engage in the sale of motor vehicles; and

children.label: B
children.text: takes title to, holds an ownership in, or takes physical custody of motor vehicles.
